objects.obs_comment: Magnitude estimated with 9x63B; coma diameter and tail length with 12.5T, 48x. In 9x63B nearly star-like object below 51 Peg (of same magnitude); in 12.5T, 48x highly condensed coma (with a bit less high surface brightness than on Apr. 8); tail very difficult. Perhaps patched thin cirrus had some influence on tail visibility.
